_comment: Slicer-settings rule table for M5.1 (ARCHITECTURE §3 settings/, §9 M5). Seeded by the human maintainer (drafted by the maintainer's reviewer, DECISIONS.md 2026-07-08). Values are engineering heuristics and human-tunable: agents implement the engine against this file and test its rule boundaries, but MUST NOT change values or structure — proposed rule changes go through a Blocked handoff. Walls/layer counts assume a 0.4 mm nozzle / ~0.2 mm layer FDM baseline.

semantics_locked.inputs: material family ('fdm' | 'resin' from materials.json), worst_case_stress_margin (dimensionless, see margin_definition), max_part_dimension_mm (largest bounding-box edge of the variant mesh).
semantics_locked.margin_definition: worst_case_stress_margin = min(in_plane_margin, interlayer_margin) for the CHOSEN build orientation, where in_plane_margin = yield_strength_mpa / max_von_mises_stress and interlayer_margin = (z_knockdown * yield_strength_mpa) / max_interlayer_tension (M4.4 field, chosen build_dir). Resin (z_knockdown = 1): the two coincide. The engine consumes the number; computing it is pipeline wiring (M5.2/M5.3), and this definition is locked so both sides agree.
semantics_locked.evaluation_order: family gate -> first margin band whose margin_below exceeds the input margin (bands are ordered ascending; the last band has margin_below = null meaning +infinity) -> size modifiers (additive) -> clamps. Deterministic; no other inputs.
semantics_locked.band_boundary_rule: A margin exactly equal to a band's margin_below belongs to the NEXT band (bands are [previous, margin_below) half-open). Engine tests must pin this boundary behavior.

margin_bands.warning: Predicted stress margin is below 1.2x even at maximum reinforcement - this variant may fail under the specified load. Consider a higher volume fraction variant, a stronger material, or revised loads.

fdm._rationale: Walls carry most of the load in FDM parts, so bands raise walls before infill. Gyroid is near-isotropic and self-supporting, hence the structural default; grid is cheaper to print and acceptable for high-margin parts. Large parts get +1 top layer (bridging over sparse infill) and +1 wall (long moment arms); small parts get +5% infill because it costs little absolute material.

resin.note: Walls/top/bottom/infill are FDM concepts; SLA/DLP parts print effectively solid. The engine returns solid-print guidance and no FDM fields.

margin_bands.warning: Predicted stress margin is below 1.5x. Standard resins are brittle (yield here is effectively fracture); consider a tough resin or a higher volume fraction variant.

min_feature_warning._comment: M5.2b: report-only, never gates or modifies geometry.
min_feature_warning.template: {count} region(s) of this variant may be thinner than 2 voxels (~reliably printable width at this resolution). Consider a higher volume fraction variant, finer voxel resolution, or a smaller nozzle.
